ocr.text: The table presents various genes along with their respective scores, descriptions, supporting evidence, and PubMed IDs. The
gene HAND2 scores 6.87 and is described as a heart- and neural crest derivatives-expressed protein 2, essential for cardiac
morphogenesis, particularly for the formation of the right ventricle and the aortic arch arteries, supported by a population study
citing various mutations found in TOF patients, with PubMed ID 20819618. FOS, with a score of 4.09, is a nuclear
phosphoprotein interacting with JUN/AP-1 transcription factor, critically regulating the development of cells destined to
maintain the skeleton, as per literature text-mining indicating cardiac hypertrophy, supported by PubMed IDs 16696897,
10328763, 12713689, and 16259952. NOTCH2, scoring 2.5, functions as a receptor for membrane-bound ligands Jagged1,
Jagged2, and Delta1 to regulate cell-fate determination, with supporting evidence from a population study of various mutations
found in CHD patients, noted under PubMed ID 16773578.
Gene  Score  Description (GeneCards Version 3)  Supporting evidence  PubMed ID

HAND2  6.87  heart- and neural crest derivatives-expressed protein 2, essential for cardiac morphogenesis, particularly for the formation of the right ventricle and of the aortic arch arteries  population study: various mutations found in TOF patients  20819618

FOS  4.09  Nuclear phosphoprotein which interacts JUN/AP-1 transcription factor. Has a critical function in regulating the development of cells destined to form and maintain the skeleton.  literature text-mining: cardiac hypertrophy  16696897, 10328763, 12713689, 16259952
NOTCH2  2.5  Functions as a receptor for membrane-bound ligands Jagged1, Jagged2 and Delta1 to regulate cell-fate determination.  population study: various mutations found in CHD patients  16773578

MLLT4  1.72  Belongs to an adhesion system which plays a role in the organization of cell-cell adherens junctions (AJs). Nectin- and actin-filament-binding protein that connects nectin to the actin cytoskeleton  similar functions to FLNA [28,29]: actin cytoskeleton, and to GJA1 [27]: cell-cell adhensions  see reference of supporting evidence
THBS1  1.7  Adhesive glycoprotein that mediates cell-to-cell and cell-to-matrix interactions  genetic association database: myocardial infarct  12482844, 16684956

MAPK14  1.69  act as an integration point for multiple biochemical signals, and are involved in a wide variety of cellular processes such as proliferation, differentiation, transcription regulation and development.  literature text-mining: pulmonary disease chronic obstructive  19880675, 17959643, 20093202, 19004925

ELK1  1.56  Can form a ternary complex with the serum response factor and the ETS and SRF motifs of the fos serum response element  SRF [4] is a cardiac-tissue enriched transcription factor that regulate many cardiac effector genes during embryogenesis  see reference of supporting evidence
MAPKAPK5  1.54  similar to MAPK14

DCN  1.52  This protein is a component of connective tissue, binds to type I collagen fibrils, and plays a role in matrix assembly.  literature text-mining: myocardial infarction, heart failure, congenital malformation, vascular diseases  17558846, 9162605, 18514055, 9493904
NUMB  1.49  plays a role in the determination of cell fates during development. associate with disease gene NOTCH1  MGI database: targeted knock-out in mice affect cardiovascular systems  11412999
MLLT4 scores 1.72 and is part of an adhesion system involved in cell-cell adherens junctions, with supporting evidence
paralleling functions to FLNA and GJA1, citing references. THBS1 scores 1.7, mediating cell-to-cell and cell-to-matrix
interactions, with genetic associations related to myocardial infarct, supported by PubMed IDs 12482844 and 16684956.
MAPK14, scoring 1.69, integrates multiple biochemical signals affecting various cellular processes, with literature text-mining
supporting pulmonary disease chronic obstructive, cited under PubMed IDs 19880675, 17959643, 20093202, and 19004925.
ELK1, scoring 1.56, forms a ternary complex with serum response factors, with supporting evidence referenced. MAPKAPK5
scores 1.54, similar to MAPK14. DCN, with a score of 1.52, is a connective tissue component binding type I collagen fibrils,
supported by literature text-mining related to myocardial infarction, heart failure, congenital malformation, and vascular
diseases, noted under PubMed IDs 17558846, 9162605, 18514055, and 9493904. Finally, NUMB scores 1.49, playing a role in
cell fate determination during development, associated with disease gene NOTCH1, with supporting evidence from the MGI
database regarding targeted knock-out in mice affecting cardiovascular systems, cited under PubMed ID 11412999.
